items.title: Opera gains ground among iPhone users in the US and UK
items.summary: Opera says its iOS browser saw monthly active users grow 93% in the UK and 50% in the US during Q2 2026 compared with the same period last year. Here are the details.

items.title: Elon Musk quietly buys a $1 billion gas turbine company to power Grok

items.title: OpenAI's first device sounds more like an Alexa beater than an iPhone killer
items.summary: A new report says OpenAI wants to produce a super-smart, "humanlike" portable speaker that gets to know you (and actually moves). (via Cult of Mac - Your source for the latest Apple news, rumors, analysis, reviews, how-tos and deals. )

items.title: Lorde says AI glasses are 'not sexy'
items.summary: "Increasingly in our world, it gets harder and harder to know what is real," Lorde said onstage.

items.title: US gov't allows Chinese telecom giant ZTE to purchase Nvidia H200 AI chips - firm joins Alibaba, Tencent, and ByteDance in access to Hopper tech
items.summary: The United States has licensed Chinese telecom giant ZTE to purchase restricted Nvidia H200 AI chips, but Chinese regulators and domestic procurement initiatives may limit the material impact of the change.
